column1: # HK colloquial characters — highest-priority overrides for canto-g2p
# These common oral/written Cantonese particles are curated manually to ensure
# correct colloquial readings, overriding rime-cantonese and Unihan where needed.
# ── Multi-char phrase fixes (fraction reading of 分) ──────────────────────────
# 分之 in fraction context is fan6 (part/portion), not fan1 (minute).
# Adding as word-level override so M分之N segments correctly.
# ── Multi-char phrase fixes (HKCanCor corpus tone-mining spike, 2026-07-20) ──
# rime-cantonese lists 麻雀 as a rime tie (maa4 zoek2 / maa4 zoek3) and
# ToJyutping's own ranking resolves it to zoek3 (citation/"sparrow" tone);
# HKCanCor's transcribed speech shows zoek2 (mahjong) is the actual usage.
# 老豆 ("dad", slang): both rime-cantonese and ToJyutping only have the
# citation reading dau6; HKCanCor shows dau2 in every colloquial occurrence.
# 雀仔 ("bird"/genital slang): rime-cantonese's only entry is the citation
# reading zoek3 zai2; HKCanCor shows zoek2 zai2 (same colloquial "zoek2"
# reading as 麻雀/孔雀 above) in every occurrence.
# ── rime-cantonese mis-tagged 唔/滴 word entries (2026-07-24) ─────────────────
# These are plain data-entry errors in rime-cantonese's jyut6ping3.words.dict,
# not colloquial-tone variants: 唔 (negation particle) has no legitimate ng-
# reading, and these words' 滴 syllable is tagged with the wrong homograph
# reading. Cross-verified against ToJyutping (CanCLID, independent source) —
# every row below matches ToJyutping's get_jyutping_list() output exactly.
# Contrast with 差唔耐 (caa1 ng4 noi6), which rime-cantonese AND ToJyutping
# both agree is a genuine idiom-specific ng4 reading — left untouched.

column2: m4 sik6 jan4 gaan1 jin1 fo2
waak6 gung1 zai2 m4 sai2 waak6 ceot1 coeng2
waak6 gung1 zai2 m4 sai2 waak6 ceot1 coeng2
dou3 diu3 mou5 dik6 mak6 seoi2

column1: # 返 (bare, no word-dict match): rime-cantonese and ToJyutping both rank
# faan2 (書面「返回」sense) ahead of faan1 (口語「翻去/翻嚟」sense) for the
# solo char-fallback case. In spoken HK Cantonese, bare 返 with no following
# object — after negation (佢未返/佢冇返), a time question (幾時返/幾點返),
# an aspect marker (佢返未/佢已經返咗), or a time adverb (我遲啲返) — is
# overwhelmingly the faan1 "go/come back" sense; faan2 rarely stands alone.
# Multi-char words that ARE faan2 (返回/返港/返臺/返抵) already have their own
# word-dict entries and are unaffected by this char-level default flip.
# NOTE: unlike the 唔/滴 fixes above, this is not corpus-verified (no local
# HKCanCor access this session) — a judgment call from common bare-verb
# sentence patterns, not a source-data contradiction. Revisit with HKCanCor
# if this turns out too broad.
# Formal-register 返 (faan2) compounds — NONE of these existed as word-dict
# entries before (neither rime-cantonese nor ToJyutping's trie), so they were
# silently relying on the same char-level default just flipped above (返 +
# faan2-by-default). Protecting them explicitly as their own word entries so
# longest-match segmentation finds the whole word before ever falling back to
# solo 返. This list is a closed, enumerable set of real formal vocabulary —
# not an attempt to cover every conceivable 返X compound. 返老還童/返璞歸真/
# 迴光返照/回光返照 already had their own rime-cantonese entries and needed no
# 返鄉(HK usage is really 返鄉下, colloquial)/返修(not standard vocabulary)/
# 返場(騷業用語「叫返個表演者出返嚟」,口語 faan1)/返本(投資術語「幾時返本」,
# 口語 faan1)/返城(HK 罕用,就算用都係口語「翻城」義) were considered and
# rejected — they fall through to the bare-返 faan1 default above instead.
